heading: Medal of honor; Air Force cross; distinguished-service medal: delegation of power to award
label: 9275 Medal of honor; Air Force cross; distinguished-service medal: delegation of power to award
text: The President may delegate his authority to award the medal of honor, Air Force cross, and distinguished-service medal, to a commanding general of a separate air force or higher unit in the field, or to an equivalent commander of a separate space force or higher unit in the field.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevised sectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)874510:1410.(16th par., less words after semicolon, under “Ordnance Department”), .July 9, 1918, ch. 14340 Stat. 872The words “under such conditions, regulations, and limitations as he shall prescribe” are omitted as surplusage. The words “his authority” are substituted for the words “the power conferred upon him by sections 1403, 1406–1408, 1409–1412, 1416, 1420, 1422, 1423, and 1424 of this title”.

Amendments2021— inserted before period at end “, or to an equivalent commander of a separate space force or higher unit in the field”.Pub. L. 116–2832018— renumbered as this section.Pub. L. 115–232section 8745 of this title1960— substituted “Air Force cross” for “distinguished-service cross” in section catchline and in text.Pub. L. 86–593

Effective Date of 2018 AmendmentAmendment by effective , with provision for the coordination of amendments and special rule for certain redesignations, see , set out as a note preceding .Pub. L. 115–232section 800 of Pub. L. 115–232section 3001 of this titleFeb. 1, 2019
